item.title: New theme for the website
item.description: Finally got round to making a new theme for this website. I figured it was well-needed to learn the ins and outs of Hugo a bit better. Over the next few days, I plan to: Add the (planned) missing features. Refine the theme. Make the theme live. Update the projects/CV sections. Publish the new content as well. The plan is to update the theme yearly to keep up to date with latest best practices.

item.title: Beache's tea (2)
item.description: But Beache can’t drink any tea, As it looks like a sheppa’s pee. They have no manners, that is true, Wriggling pgs turn Beache blue. It is Beache who needs a mend. The sheppas drove her off the bend. Now the sight, smell, sound of wee, Beache has PTSD But no matter, she has Cuby, Super Bunny will set her free. Now there’s no more bad memories, Oh, sweet revenging when upping flies!

item.title: Beache's tea (1)
item.description: Sheppas are just such a pain Beache says with much disdain Look at em all over me While I try to drink my tea They got no manners or class Always going for nut bars They need help, the mental kind Which I am willing to find Sad to say that in the end They all say don’t need a mend Beache’s tea is cold and done All spoiled for sheppas' fun

item.title: Sugar (2)
item.description: But Bouncy isn’t cute Beache is not a brute Kissing his damaged cheeks Helping them heal in weeks Don’t give sugar now Don’t make dat Beache bow He’ll do his pervy deeds No one wants his love seeds

item.title: Sugar (1)
item.description: Bouncy looks super cute Beache is just a brute Tainting his rosy cheeks Healing takes weeks and weeks. But give him sugar now Then make dat Beache bow He will forgive those deeds And sow some love as seeds

item.title: Rubbish ball
item.description: Sheppas are so freaking cute They fit in a rubbish chute You can whack em like a ball And they’ll bounce right off the wall

item.title: Mistletoe
item.description: Bouncy kisses mistletoe Thinking it was the Yoyo Yoyo looked so unamused Bouncy ended up so bruised Yoyo wanted chippizi At the cost of more acne Bouncy snapped up at the chance And was beat back all at once Bouncy was sent to ED As his love was not to be Cuby gave him a lolly Soon enough he was jolly

item.title: Pg Pie (2)
item.description: Note: PG pie (1) has been lost due to the stream of time. I am honest, I don’t lie I don’t like dat pg pie Lock em up until they stop Maybe till their pgs flow Beating their pgs is gross It’s like picking sheppa nose Thus sheppa pgs are fine While the rest is hit times nine So I guess that in the end That the Beache was no mend

item.title: Host Club
item.description: Background Based on a game called Host no Abunai Sekai (ホストのアブナイ世界), which roughly translates to “The Dangerous World of Hosts”. I didn’t understand a word of it, but it was surprisingly fun. Yes, even if you didn’t understand a word of it. The story starts with the player (you) getting scammed by a loan shark. Then you start running a host club where you scam other women in order to pay back that loan.

item.title: Thoughts on replacing my laptop
item.description: I’ve been using a Macbook Air Mid-2013 from, well, 2013. It was my first high quality laptop, and one that was the state of art at the time. From the moment I got this laptop, I thought, I’m now married to my laptop. But as I grow up, I realise that…maybe I’ve just grown out of it. Maybe it’s because 4 GB RAM doesn’t do much apart from browsing the web.

item.title: Sometimes there's just too much stuff
item.description: Too much you want to do and not enough time. Too much on your plate and not enough what you want. Such a paiiiiin….

item.title: Kawaii sesame
item.description: Beache sold the sesame Coz it was not kawaii Sheppa crowds piled outside They needed their doorbell guide When Cuby got stuck as well Angry she was, in nutshell Sesame bell was brought back And the crowds were back on track

item.title: Home vs. work
item.description: How I wish to go home now I am so bored, bored somehow How I miss my Cuby smirk Much better than doing work

item.title: Sheppa chop shop
item.description: Beache sells a sheppa head Sheppas fall right out of bed As they shiver in the night Beache wakes them for a fight But tis a losing battle Round up as sheppa cattle Grieving for lost innocence Due to Beache’s wickedness Beache sells more sheppa parts How it chills their sheppa hearts Tastier, as Beache said Sheppa panic was widespread Beache claims she’ll hit a few Some sheppas will go in stew

item.title: Sheppas down
item.description: Sheppas scrabble day and night, Diving right in with delight. Cuby gives sheppas a frown - Time for sheppas to go down. All gifted back as slaves, Sheppas sigh and carve their graves. Beache smirks with boastful glee, Watching sheppas scram and flee. But this time Beache “plays fair”, Giving sheppas work to share: As long as all work is done, Extra time is left for fun. Sadly there are selfish punks,

item.title: Selling sheppas (2)
item.description: Sheppas forced to work all day For Beache with no sick pay Against Beache they riot Beache knocks them out one shot More sheppas rise in their place Stopping Beache is a race Beache hits them with a smirk Tossing them with a knee jerk They slowly start to stack up Can’t seem em unless closeup Sheppas pile nice and high Beache prices her supply Cuby frowns at the carpet

item.title: Selling sheppas (1)
item.description: Next door to the sheppa shop, Opens a store, selling chop. Where do parts originate? From sheppas out on a date. It becomes the new food trend, Sheppas' spirits start to bend. They serve Beache head to toe, Fearing to become her foe. Beache sits back on her chair, Sitting with a queenly air. Sheppas pool around her feet, Sighing/crying in defeat. At the store of sheppa chop, Slowly sales start to drop,

item.title: Kind beache (2)
item.description: The Beache sits on a chair Sheppas ignore her like air So she thinks to make a blend Made of sheppas, a new trend The sheppas run for their lives Into hidey holes in hives Beache bangs hard on the walls Which drop like heavy rainfalls

item.title: Kind Beache (1)
item.description: He fumes to “friends” in the group, They laugh and call him a dupe. “You fools are so mean!” He cries, All alone, just filled with sighs. Then a kind Beache appears, Behind her back, hiding shears, “Beache comfort me!” He whines, No doubt missing all the signs….

item.title: Beache Flea
item.description: Every sheppa thinks Beache Is a nasty evil flea Sheppas try hard for their gain It will always be in vain This sheppa will not end well It will crack like an eggshell As it goes under a knife Begging will not save its life

item.title: Big scratchy
item.description: There was a young hamster called Beache, Who thought eating sheppas was catchy… She grabbed one to kiss, But he gave a hiss, And gave her a super big scratchy!

item.title: Random (1)
item.description: Cuby left her hidey hole In the rain she took a stroll Laughing about fake trump cards Of which Beache disregards Now Cuby is sad and cold Sheppas are left uncontrolled Time for Beache to go home Sheppas shall no longer roam

item.title: Cold legume (4)
item.description: Chompy, chowy, chew, chum, My, this legume sure is yum. Why is it rainy today, Me mood is already gray…. I must get up for me class, Why are mornings always harsh? Me still luckier than you, Except when wet and cold too.

item.title: Cold legume (3)
item.description: Beache wants some food But cold makes bad mood If Cuby comes now She will surely chow

item.title: Cold legume (2)
item.description: Cuby huddles in blankets, Sniffing/sneezing like trumpets. If she eats that cold legume, Will they be in the same room?

item.title: Cold legume (1)
item.description: Beache wants to go home now But the world will not allow So she shivers in a room Becoming a cold legume

item.title: Missing coat (2)
item.description: Cuby lounges back in bed, Nothing sticking in her head. Woken up by drumming rain, Now trying to sleep in vain. Reading Beache’s sad email, Cuby takes a big inhale. She hopes the coat is alright, Not taken without a fight. I will try to hurry back. When we arrive, hit the track, Speed towards your waiting friend. This story needs a good end.

item.title: Missing coat (1)
item.description: The skies are so full with doom Beache is sitting in gloom She forgot her duffel coat Hopes that it will not be sought

item.title: Pg here!
item.description: PGPGPG scrub Rub a dub a PG rub. No need to be so so sad, I am here, you should be glad!

item.title: Alone (2)
item.description: Sadly Beache is at work An annoyed and sleepy clerk Might as well wait till the end As the evil recommend

item.title: Alone (1)
item.description: Oh not again, splish, splash, splosh Well there’s no need to be posh. When I am left all alone, No one hears my whine and moan.

item.title: Study war
item.description: Cuby are you doing work Coz good sheppas have their perk But oops! There is no Beache Who will let you be carefree Later Beache will be nice To sheppas who learn precise They will get some fruit before They go start their study war

item.title: Sleepy (2)
item.description: Cuby will stress for the tests Yet do better than suggests Cuby will do really well Say it was a lucky spell Cuby will then slack again Drool over hot sheppa men As the next semester dawns Her lectures will fill with yawns

item.title: Sleepy (1)
item.description: I am sleepy sleepified, Too lost and behind! I cried. Writing, listening, I do try, but straight into dreams I fly. As classes comes to an end, My consciousness rounds the bend. Bright-eyed and bushy tailed, That’s when I know I failed.

item.title: Bullying sheppas (1)
item.description: While it is still unaware I will throw it in the air Watch it come down with a splat Make it into a doormat As the sheppa whines and cries I will give its next surprise I will chuck it in the bins Cloud its skies with evil grins I get bored and go away It go finds its fiance(e) Someone will then give a fart One that pulls their hearts apart

item.title: About Me & This Site
item.description: I kinda like to work (min 46 hours per week), but I have a slightly potty mouth that complains about life a lot. My mouth is pottier under stress - so much that my supervisor calls me a grumpy cat - but then my colleagues discuss their ideas to help me. I tend to value speed over accuracy (get the stuff over and done with and then make sure it’s right).

item.title: Contact
item.description: Loading…

item.title: Reporting Portal for Clients
item.description: Background Businesses like data. B2B businesses especially like to see the ins and outs of stock. And money doesn’t always go from client > business > supplier while goods go the other way. It can be reversed. A reporting portal for the client and supplier is always a good way to manage that complexity. The original reporting portal was a bit clunky from old code and outdated designs. Objectives Replicate original portal Allow clients to access supplier portals and vice versa Allow use of dynamic graphs Improve design of original portal Main Tasks Replicate reports from original portal Allow clients to hop across multiple databases to access previously inaccessible reports Allow easy creation of new graphs for visual display of data Improve security & reduce bugs Improve UI/UX Approach Language/Framework: Originally I tried to use Wordpress.

item.title: Screw Accounting
item.description: Background I am a lazy person. Data-entry may have been in my job description, but I absolutely hate doing it. Why? It’s so freaking repetitive and it’s way too easy to mess up. When I switched to working from home, motivation to these boring repetitive tasks plummeted even more. And then there were even more time constraints after I decided to go back to study and I still had to work through a huge backlog of non-data-entry tasks.

item.title: Warehouse Transfer App
item.description: Background When items get transferred within the warehouse, their location records in the database are updated so people know where to find ‘em. The full company app is simply too complicated to lug around the warehouse, so a slim version of it is used. It was a pain to use. The slim version had sizes that transformed quite terribly onto tablets used for scanning. Buttons would disappear into the middle of nowhere.
